top_signals.title: Global investors remain confident in Gulf economy after US-Iran deal
top_signals.summary: Global investors remain confident in the Gulf’s economic outlook despite regional tensions with over 71 per cent expecting a negotiated agreement to end the US-Iran conflict. Active investors across the US, UK, Germany, France and China found that 82 per cent were confident about the Gulf’s future economic outlook, while 69 percent said the region ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: China’s H1 Crude Imports Post Sharpest Drop Since War Began
top_signals.summary: Chinese seaborne crude oil imports fell approximately 23% year-on-year in the first half of 2026, according to Signal Ocean vessel-tracking data, declining from roughly 1.87 billion barrels in H1 2025 to an estimated 1.44 billion barrels in H1 2026. Imports opened the year above 2025 levels in January and February before turning sharply lower from ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Japan-linked vessels exit Strait of Hormuz after months stranded in Gulf – reports
top_signals.summary: Ten Japan-linked ships were exiting the Strait of Hormuz on Monday after being stranded in the narrow waterway for months, according to media reports. Citing shipping data from LSEG, Reuters reported that the vessels include six very large crude carriers loaded with 12 million barrels of Middle Eastern crude, as well as two chemical tankers, ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Bank of England appoints Rhys Phillips as the new Chief Cashier
top_signals.summary: The Governor of the Bank of England, Andrew Bailey, has announced that Rhys Phillips will be the next Chief Cashier and Director of Notes. He will take up the role on 19 October 2026.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Keel jumps 10% after former bitcoin miner hires data center veteran as president
top_signals.summary: Former Digital Realty executive Ganesh Aiyer will lead Keel's commercial growth and power pipeline expansion.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: H1 2026: The Moving Target of Maritime Sanctions Compliance
top_signals.summary: The first major maritime sanctions deadline of 2026 arrived on January 21, when the EU’s refined-products ban began applying to fuels produced in third countries from Russian crude. That deadline set the tone for the months that followed: more granular rules, more infrastructure-linked exposure, and more time-bound permissions that compliance teams need to track alongside ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: ZAWYA: Kuwait targets 4 bn/cfd gas output
top_signals.summary: The state-owned Kuwait Oil Company (KOC) is pushing ahead with plans to increase production of associated and non-associated gas to a total of 4 billion cubic feet per day over the next ten years, oil sources in the emirate reveal. KOC’s gas operations cover a vast geographical area with pipelines totaling more than 5,000 kilometres ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: BP sells stake in Canada offshore oil project to Equinor
top_signals.summary: BP agreed to sell its stake in the Bay du Nord offshore oil project in Canada to partner Equinor, the companies said on Monday. Financial terms of the transaction were not disclosed. Equinor will become the sole owner of Bay du Nord after acquiring BP’s 37.2% stake. The Norwegian energy company plans to advance the ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Dry Bulk Market: India’s Coal Import Puzzle
top_signals.summary: Demand for bulkers to service India’s coal imports could be on the move over the next few months. In a recent weekly report, shipbroker Intermodal said that “India’s coal trade is moving in two different directions. Thermal coal imports are lower, while Russian coal arrivals are increasing. For dry bulk, the impact depends on which ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: China now buys 61% of the world’s traded soybeans
top_signals.summary: China’s growing reliance on imported food, South America’s expanding dominance in agricultural exports and mounting geopolitical risks around fertiliser supply are reshaping global agricultural trade and will lead to 2.3% growth in global agricultural trade, according to the latest Agri Markets 360 report launched today (6 July) by shipbroker IFCHOR GALBRAITHS. The new annual research ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Canadian spy agency reports hacking three criminal groups in 2025
top_signals.summary: A ransomware-as-a-service gang, an online foreign extremist group and drug traffickers were separately the targets of offensive operations in 2025, according to Canada's Communications Security Establishment.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Waller, Two Thoughts on the Transmission of Monetary Policy
top_signals.summary: Speech At "Challenges for Monetary Policy Transmission in a Changing World," a conference sponsored by the Bank of Italy for the research network initiated by the European System of Central Banks, Rome, Italy

alerts.title: Victims mourned 8 years after western Japan rain disaster
alerts.summary: Residents across the Chugoku region on Monday mourned over 300 people who lost their lives in the incident that hit the area in 2018.
alerts.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

alerts.title: Metered electricity demand in the New York ISO falls midday because of small-scale solar
alerts.summary: An increase in electricity generation from small-scale solar in New York has decreased the midday demand for metered electricity, amid overall declining load in the state. The trend is particularly notable in the early spring (March and April), when solar generation has an outsized impact because demand is relatively low and conditions for solar generation are favorable.
